So 1994's 3% pullback and 2022's 33% decline both came from the Fed hiking rates and the comp's current setup, three straight years of 20% plus year gains after a downyear has only happened twice before. Um so the first was after 1994 is 3% decline then you had 1995 1996 and 1997 all delivered 20% plus years 989 of course kept going of 40 and 68% before the dot finally uh arrived in 2000. The second was after 2018's 4% decline. You had 2019, 2020, and 2021 all deliver 20% plus year uh 20% plus years. Then 2022 brought the Fed driven bare market. So again, um so the takeaway here is that history says the NASDAQ should keep rallying beyond this year, barring of course that Fed rate shock. Uh there will be more pullbacks like in any bull market, but we do continue to treat them as buying opportunities.
